DISCHARGE SUMMARY

Patient: Jennifer Williams
DOB: 02/07/2017
191632525089
Admitted: 12/03/2016
Discharged: 10/25/2024
Attending: Dr. Amy Lawson

Mr. Patrick is a 61 male admitted to Watkins PLC on
Aug 21, 2017 with chest pain and shortness of breath. Past medical history
significant for hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and CAD. Wife Nancy was at
bedside throughout admission.

Discharge disposition: home with home health.
Follow-up scheduled with Dr. Elaine on 02/17/2023.
Patient may be reached at (226)983-7660 or agreen@example.com.

document.text:
PROGRESS NOTE — 2018-06-09

Patient Jessica Thomas, 88, presents to clinic for follow-up of recently
diagnosed sarcoidosis. Seen by Dr. Marie Jacob in pulmonology last
Thursday. Patient lives at 3373 Patricia Estate Suite 572, Debra 42711 Dominguez Square.
Husband Brandon called the front desk on 11/30/2024 with concerns about new
shortness of breath.

Currently on prednisone 40mg daily. CBC drawn this AM, CMP pending.
Patient's mother Thomas was diagnosed with the same condition in 1994 at
33. Family history otherwise non-contributory.

Plan: continue current regimen, follow-up in 2 weeks. Patient reachable at
0020236149.

document.text:
NURSING NOTE 0730

Pt seen this AM for am care. Roberts continues to do well post-op day 3.
States pain controlled with PCA, last bolus at 0612. Daughter (Margaret
Roberts-Hennessy) at bedside, stayed overnight. Spouse Henry called the
unit at 0445 from work and will visit this afternoon after his shift at
WakeMed Cary ends.

Vitals stable: BP 128/76, HR 82, T 98.4, RR 16, SpO2 97% RA. Incision C/D/I.
Foley draining clear yellow urine. Bowel sounds present in all 4 quads.
Tolerating clear liquids — advanced to soft diet at breakfast per Dr.
Okonkwo's order yesterday.

PT eval scheduled with Janet today; case management contacted re: discharge
planning, likely going home Friday with VNA. Insurance auth pending — see
note from Maria in case mgmt dated 4/21/25.

Hospital ID: WMH-2847-0419-A. Pager 919-555-0287. Email update sent to
attending at j.okonkwo@hospital.org.

ED TRIAGE — [DATE]

Name: [NAME], DOB [DATE], [ID].
Chief complaint: left ankle injury after fall on ice. Arrived by private
vehicle with friend [NAME] (contact [PHONE]).

Triage: pain [DATE], neurovascular intact. X-ray order placed. Placed in
Rapid Track bay 4. PCP: Dr. [NAME] at [HOSPITAL],
next appt [DATE].

HISTORY & PHYSICAL (EXCERPT)

Admitting: Dr. L. Maria for internal medicine. Patient: Austin Stewart, 35, 41596198126, SS# ***-**-4102 (verified from chart).

History obtained from patient and daughter Jessica Kelley (001-874-598-2141x445, same household). Allergies: penicillin (rash). Home meds
reviewed with patient; furosemide dose confirmed.

Social: retired electrician, 1 ppd x30y (quit 2019), occasional ETOH. Lives
at 01794 Olsen Junction, Andrew NC 79812.

document.text:
MEDICATION RECONCILIATION — PRE-OP

Verified with patient: metformin 1000mg BID, lisinopril 10mg daily,
atorvastatin 40mg QHS. Stopped: aspirin per cardiology (Dr. S. Okafor,
faxed 4/10/25).

Pharmacy on file: MedStar Pharmacy, 100 Trade St, Raleigh; phone
984-555-1200. Secondary insurance card on file, member ID ZZ8849302.

Pre-op call sheet lists emergency contact: brother Paolo Ricci, email
p.ricci77@proton.me, best number 252-555-4401.

RADIOLOGY — CHEST CTA, FINAL

Indication: Rule out PE, dyspnea. Patient: Keisha [NAME], [DATE], [ID]. Comparison: chest radiograph [DATE].

Impression: No evidence of acute pulmonary embolus. Scattered
ground-glass, possibly infectious vs inflammatory. Discussed with covering
hospitalist Dr. [NAME]; critical result logged at [DATE_TIME] and paged
[PHONE] per protocol.

Dictated: A. [NAME] MD, [DATE] [DATE]. Electronic signature on file.
Reading location: [ORGANIZATION] Imaging Plaza, [ID] Science Dr.
